summary: Defines the number structure and functionality for international public telecommunication numbers: digits only, a maximum length of 15 digits, and a country code of 1 to 3 digits followed by a national (significant) number. Covers the five number categories (geographic areas, global services, networks, groups of countries, and trials) and the digit analysis needed to route calls. This in-force edition was approved 13 February 2026, superseding the 11/2010 edition.
title: The international public telecommunication numbering plan
